rawFindings.title: Missing HSTS Header
rawFindings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security header is missing. This allows downgrade attacks and cookie hijacking.

rawFindings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
rawFindings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header. Browsers may MIME-sniff responses. Note: Target uses Cloudflare which may inject this header at the platform level.

rawFindings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
rawFindings.description: No Referrer-Policy set. Sensitive data in URLs may leak via the Referer header. Note: Target uses Cloudflare which may inject this header at the platform level.

rawFindings.title: HTTP TRACE Method Enabled
rawFindings.description: The HTTP TRACE method is enabled on the server. This can be exploited for Cross-Site Tracing (XST) attacks to steal credentials.
rawFindings.evidence: Template trace-method-enabled matched: HTTP TRACE Method Enabled

rawFindings.title: X-Powered-By Header Exposes Technology
rawFindings.description: The X-Powered-By response header exposes the server technology and version. Remove this header to reduce information leakage.
rawFindings.evidence: Template x-powered-by-exposed matched: X-Powered-By Header Exposes Technology

rawFindings.title: Missing Subresource Integrity (SRI)
rawFindings.description: Found 1 external resource(s) without integrity attributes on https://app.cal.com/login. Without SRI, if the CDN or external host is compromised, an attacker can inject malicious code that the browser will execute without verification. Add integrity="sha384-..." and crossorigin="anonymous" attributes to all external script and stylesheet tags.

rawFindings.title: Source Map Exposure — Original Source Code Accessible
rawFindings.description: A JavaScript source map at https://app.cal.com/_next/static/chunks/38892383c615aecb.js.map is publicly accessible and contains the original source code of 4 file(s) via the sourcesContent field. Attackers can reconstruct the entire application source, review business logic, find hardcoded secrets, and identify vulnerabilities without needing to deobfuscate minified code.
rawFindings.evidence:
Source map URL: https://app.cal.com/_next/static/chunks/38892383c615aecb.js.map
Source files exposed: 4
sourcesContent present: YES — full original source code accessible

rawFindings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Authentication Endpoint
rawFindings.description: The authentication endpoint at /auth/login does not enforce rate limiting. An attacker can perform brute-force password attacks, credential stuffing, or account enumeration without being throttled.
rawFindings.evidence: Sent 15 rapid requests — all returned HTTP 200 with no rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-*, Retry-After, etc.) and no 429 response.

rawFindings.title: Race Condition on Page /register
rawFindings.description:
Sent 10 concurrent GET requests to /register.
- 10/10 requests succeeded (expected at most 1)

This may allow double-spend, coupon reuse, vote manipulation, or other TOCTOU attacks.
The endpoint should use database-level locks, idempotency keys, or optimistic concurrency control.
rawFindings.evidence:
Concurrent requests: 10
Successful responses: 10/10
Status codes: 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200
Unique response bodies: 1
Average response time: 1017ms
All responses identical (no concurrency control detected)

Race indicators:
  - 10/10 requests succeeded (expected at most 1)

interpretedFindings.title: Missing HSTS Header
interpretedFindings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security header is missing. This allows downgrade attacks and cookie hijacking.
interpretedFindings.impact: Missing security headers reduce defense-in-depth, making other attacks easier to exploit.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: Header "strict-transport-security" not present in response
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Add the recommended security headers to your web server or application middleware configuration.

interpretedFindings.title: Web Cache Deception via /nonexistent.css on /admin/30min
interpretedFindings.description: Appending "/nonexistent.css" to /admin/30min returns the original dynamic page content, and the response contains cache headers suggesting the CDN treats it as a static asset. Manual verification recommended to confirm cached content is accessible. The page contains user-specific content (PII/tokens/session data) which could be stolen by any visitor.
interpretedFindings.impact: An attacker can poison the cache with a crafted response, causing all subsequent visitors to receive malicious content served from the cache — enabling XSS, phishing, or credential theft at scale.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Ensure all user-controlled headers that affect the response are included in the cache key. Validate and normalize Host and forwarding headers server-side. Use Vary headers correctly and consider using cache-busting techniques for sensitive endpoints.

interpretedFindings.title: Possible XPath Injection — Boolean-Based (month parameter)
interpretedFindings.description: The parameter "month" at https://app.cal.com/admin/30min?month=2026-04 shows different responses for XPath tautology (469123 chars) vs contradiction (388645 chars), suggesting user input controls XPath query logic.
interpretedFindings.impact: An attacker can read, modify, or delete database contents, potentially taking full control of the application.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: {"payloadUsed":"Tautology: ' or '1'='1 vs Contradiction: ' and '1'='2","responseIndicators":["Tautology response: 469123 chars (status 200)","Contradiction response: 388645 chars (status 200)","Difference: 80478 chars (17%)"],"httpExchange":{"request":{"method":"GET","url":"https://app.cal.com/admin/30min?month=%27+or+%271%27%3D%271"},"response":{"status":200,"headers":{},"bodySnippet":"Boolean difference: 80478 chars"}}}
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL.

interpretedFindings.title: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection — parameter-entity
interpretedFindings.description: The endpoint accepts XML input and is vulnerable to XXE injection. Parameter entity XXE — uses % entity for blind detection. An attacker can read arbitrary files from the server, perform SSRF, or cause denial of service.
interpretedFindings.impact: An attacker can read arbitrary files from the server, perform SSRF attacks to internal services, or cause denial of service via entity expansion (billion laughs). With out-of-band techniques, even blind XXE can exfiltrate data.
reproductionSteps:
3. Evidence: Technique: parameter-entity
Description: Parameter entity XXE — uses % entity for blind detection
Detection: XXE payload "parameter-entity" succeeded — response matches indicator: /root:.*:0:0|\/bin\/(ba)?sh|error|DTD/i
Response status: 403
Response snippet: <!DOCTYPE html>
<!--[if lt IE 7]> <html class="no-js ie6 oldie" lang="en-US"> <![endif]-->
<!--[if IE 7]>    <html class="no-js ie7 oldie" lang="en-US"> <![endif]-->
<!--[if IE 8]>    <html class="no-js ie8 oldie" lang="en-US"> <![endif]-->
<!--[if gt IE 8]><!--> <html class="no-js" lang="en-US"> <!
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Disable external entity processing in the XML parser configuration. In Java: setFeature("http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl", true). In PHP: libxml_disable_entity_loader(true). In .NET: XmlReaderSettings.DtdProcessing = DtdProcessing.Prohibit. Use JSON instead of XML where possible.

  -X POST \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/xml' \
  -d '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [

  %xxe;
]>
<root>test</root>' \
  -L \
  -i \
  'https://app.cal.com/api/trpc/features/map'

interpretedFindings.title: LDAP Injection — Error-Based (wildcard-filter-break)
interpretedFindings.description: The endpoint exposes LDAP error messages when given malformed input, confirming that user input is passed directly into LDAP queries. This can be exploited for authentication bypass or data extraction.
interpretedFindings.impact: An attacker can bypass authentication by injecting LDAP filter operators (wildcard, tautology), extract directory attributes (email, phone, group memberships), or enumerate valid usernames. In enterprise environments using Active Directory or OpenLDAP, this can lead to full domain compromise.
reproductionSteps:
3. Evidence: Parameter: user
Technique: wildcard-filter-break
Payload: *)(objectClass=*
Error pattern: invalid.*dn|invalid.*filter
Response status: 200
Response snippet: <!DOCTYPE html><html class="notranslate" translate="no" lang="en" dir="ltr" data-nextjs-router="app"><head nonce="Xfgx8JTLjht4TgItX6kzAg=="><meta charSet="utf-8"/><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover, user-scalable=no"/><link rel="pr
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Never concatenate user input into LDAP filter strings. Use parameterized LDAP queries or escape special characters (*, (, ), \, NUL) with the LDAP-specific encoding (\2a, \28, \29, \5c, \00). In Java use javax.naming.ldap.Rdn.escapeValue(). In PHP use ldap_escape(). In Python use ldap3.utils.conv.escape_filter_chars(). Validate input against strict allowlists for username fields.

This can bypass ACLs that only check the actual HTTP method. Baseline POST → 403, override DELETE → 403. CWE-650: Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side.
interpretedFindings.impact: Missing or insufficient access controls allow attackers to access admin endpoints, bypass authorization, or escalate privileges.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: POST https://app.cal.com/api/trpc/me/myStats with X-HTTP-Method-Override: DELETE → 403 (baseline POST → 403). Response size: baseline=5045, probe=5109.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Implement server-side authorization checks on every request, deny by default, and enforce role-based access control consistently across all endpoints.

interpretedFindings.description: The cookie "__Secure-next-auth.callback-url" is accessible via JavaScript, increasing XSS impact.
interpretedFindings.impact: Insecure cookies can be stolen or manipulated, potentially leading to session hijacking.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Set HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Strict flags on all session cookies.

interpretedFindings.title: Missing Subresource Integrity (SRI)
interpretedFindings.owaspCategory: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
interpretedFindings.description: Found 1 external resource(s) without integrity attributes on https://app.cal.com/login. Without SRI, if the CDN or external host is compromised, an attacker can inject malicious code that the browser will execute without verification. Add integrity="sha384-..." and crossorigin="anonymous" attributes to all external script and stylesheet tags.
interpretedFindings.impact: External resources loaded without integrity verification can be tampered with if the CDN or third-party host is compromised.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Add integrity attributes to all external <script> and <link> tags. Use the crossorigin="anonymous" attribute. Generate hashes with shasum or online SRI generators.

interpretedFindings.title: Source Map Exposure — Original Source Code Accessible
interpretedFindings.description: A JavaScript source map at https://app.cal.com/_next/static/chunks/38892383c615aecb.js.map is publicly accessible and contains the original source code of 4 file(s) via the sourcesContent field. Attackers can reconstruct the entire application source, review business logic, find hardcoded secrets, and identify vulnerabilities without needing to deobfuscate minified code.
interpretedFindings.impact: Exposed files such as .git, .env, or source maps reveal internal paths, credentials, and source code to attackers.
reproductionSteps:
3. Evidence: Source map URL: https://app.cal.com/_next/static/chunks/38892383c615aecb.js.map
Source files exposed: 4
sourcesContent present: YES — full original source code accessible
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Block public access to .git, .env, source maps, and backup files via web server rules and ensure sensitive files are outside the web root.

interpretedFindings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Authentication Endpoint
interpretedFindings.description: The authentication endpoint at /auth/login does not enforce rate limiting. An attacker can perform brute-force password attacks, credential stuffing, or account enumeration without being throttled.
interpretedFindings.impact: Missing rate limiting allows attackers to brute-force credentials, OTPs, or overwhelm the application with automated requests.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: Sent 15 rapid requests — all returned HTTP 200 with no rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-*, Retry-After, etc.) and no 429 response.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Implement rate limiting on authentication and sensitive API endpoints using token bucket or sliding window algorithms.

interpretedFindings.title: OAuth missing state parameter on /api/auth/session
interpretedFindings.description: OAuth authorization endpoint accepts requests without a state parameter, enabling CSRF attacks on the OAuth flow.
interpretedFindings.impact: Flaws in the OAuth flow allow attackers to perform CSRF attacks to link victim accounts, steal authorization codes via redirect_uri bypass, or harvest tokens from URL logs and Referer headers.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: Endpoint responded with 200 without requiring state parameter
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Always require and validate the state parameter in OAuth authorization requests. Enforce a strict allowlist of registered redirect_uri values. Never include access tokens in URL parameters — use response_mode=fragment only when necessary and prefer response_mode=form_post for server-side flows.

interpretedFindings.title: Race Condition on Page /register
interpretedFindings.description:
Sent 10 concurrent GET requests to /register.
- 10/10 requests succeeded (expected at most 1)

This may allow double-spend, coupon reuse, vote manipulation, or other TOCTOU attacks.
The endpoint should use database-level locks, idempotency keys, or optimistic concurrency control.
interpretedFindings.impact: Concurrent request abuse can exploit time-of-check to time-of-use gaps, causing duplicate transactions or state corruption.
reproductionSteps:
3. Evidence: Concurrent requests: 10
Successful responses: 10/10
Status codes: 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200, 200
Unique response bodies: 1
Average response time: 1017ms
All responses identical (no concurrency control detected)

Race indicators:
  - 10/10 requests succeeded (expected at most 1)
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Use database-level locking, idempotency keys, or serialized transactions to prevent concurrent request abuse on state-changing operations.

interpretedFindings.title: Username Enumeration — login form (content-based)
interpretedFindings.description: The login endpoint reveals whether a username/email exists via observable response differences. An attacker can enumerate valid accounts by comparing responses for valid vs invalid usernames. This enables targeted phishing, credential stuffing, and brute-force attacks.
interpretedFindings.impact: Exposed files such as .git, .env, or source maps reveal internal paths, credentials, and source code to attackers.
reproductionSteps:
3. Evidence: Form: https://app.cal.com/auth/login
Type: login
Username field: email
Probe username: admin
Discrepancies:
  - "User exists" pattern detected: wrong\s*password
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Block public access to .git, .env, source maps, and backup files via web server rules and ensure sensitive files are outside the web root.

interpretedFindings.title: HTTP Method Override: X-Method-Override header accepted (PUT)
interpretedFindings.description: Endpoint https://app.cal.com/api/trpc/slots/getSchedule honors the X-Method-Override: PUT header. A POST request with this header is routed as a PUT request. This can bypass ACLs that only check the actual HTTP method. Baseline POST → 404, override PUT → 403. CWE-650: Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side.
interpretedFindings.impact: Missing or insufficient access controls allow attackers to access admin endpoints, bypass authorization, or escalate privileges.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: POST https://app.cal.com/api/trpc/slots/getSchedule with X-Method-Override: PUT → 403 (baseline POST → 404). Response size: baseline=162, probe=5109.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Implement server-side authorization checks on every request, deny by default, and enforce role-based access control consistently across all endpoints.

interpretedFindings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on API Endpoint
interpretedFindings.description: The API endpoint at /api/trpc/features/map does not enforce rate limiting. This may allow abuse, scraping, or resource exhaustion attacks.
interpretedFindings.impact: Missing rate limiting allows attackers to brute-force credentials, OTPs, or overwhelm the application with automated requests.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: Sent 15 rapid requests — all returned HTTP 200 with no rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-*, Retry-After, etc.) and no 429 response.
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Implement rate limiting on authentication and sensitive API endpoints using token bucket or sliding window algorithms.

interpretedFindings.title: OAuth PKCE not enforced on /api/auth/session
interpretedFindings.description: The OAuth authorization endpoint does not require PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange). Without PKCE, authorization codes are vulnerable to interception attacks, especially on mobile/native apps.
interpretedFindings.impact: Flaws in the OAuth flow allow attackers to perform CSRF attacks to link victim accounts, steal authorization codes via redirect_uri bypass, or harvest tokens from URL logs and Referer headers.
reproductionSteps: 3. Evidence: Endpoint accepted authorization request without code_challenge parameter (HTTP 200)
interpretedFindings.suggestedFix: Always require and validate the state parameter in OAuth authorization requests. Enforce a strict allowlist of registered redirect_uri values. Never include access tokens in URL parameters — use response_mode=fragment only when necessary and prefer response_mode=form_post for server-side flows.
